codex_prompt.objective: Extend the existing RobotOps implementation with six phases that harden ingestion, data contracts, state storage, mapping, dedupe ordering, adapter harness, and UI live update efficiency. The current system uses Socket.io for UI live updates and HTTPS POST ingest into an internal queue. Do not implement MQTT in these phases unless explicitly required by a future vendor integration.

current_state_context.ingestion: HTTPS POST /ingest/telemetry into API then internal queue using NATS abstraction
current_state_context.canonical_envelope: Exists for telemetry with source, events array, robot_id, timestamp, metrics, validation and dedupe before persistence
current_state_context.ui_broadcast_payloads: Currently sends arrays of domain objects derived from database queries
current_state_context.first_real_integration_target: Vendor fleet manager API using REST or vendor WebSocket

phases.name: Lock canonical envelope into three message types
phases.goal: Formalize the internal ingestion contract into robot_state, robot_event, and task_status. Ensure every adapter and ingest path emits exactly one of these types and that validation is strict.
deliverables: Add message_type to the canonical envelope and validate it
Define schemas for robot_state payload, robot_event payload, task_status payload
Split existing events array semantics into robot_event only. Robot_state must not be bundled as events.
Update ingest endpoint and queue consumers to route by message_type
Add versioning rules with schema_version and compatibility checks
data_model_changes: Add canonical_message table for raw validated envelopes or store raw in object storage with references
Add enums for message_type and severity and category
acceptance_criteria: Ingest rejects unknown message_type
Ingest rejects payloads that do not match message_type schema
Queue consumer routes messages correctly by message_type
Robot_state updates do not create incidents unless a separate robot_event is emitted

phases.name: Implement last known state table as first class read model
phases.goal: Create a fast, stable read model for Fleet and Facility views. Stop relying on aggregating robot state from many tables per refresh.
deliverables: Create robot_last_state table keyed by tenant_id, site_id, robot_id
Upsert logic on ingest for robot_state only
Expose query endpoint GET /robots/last_state with filters for site, status, vendor, tag
Update Fleet list and Facility map to read from robot_last_state
Add robot_offline computation using last_seen_at and configured timeout
acceptance_criteria: Fleet page reads only from robot_last_state for live state
Facility page reads only from robot_last_state for live pose
Robot becomes offline after timeout if no robot_state updates arrive
Reads remain fast with large robot counts by using proper indexes

phases.name: Add site mapping and coordinate transforms
phases.goal: Handle vendor map frames and coordinate systems cleanly by introducing a mapping layer to RobotOps floorplans and a deterministic transform pipeline.
deliverables: Create vendor_site_map entity linking vendor map id or name to RobotOps floorplan_id
Implement transform pipeline that supports scale, rotation, and translation
Apply transforms during ingest so robot_last_state always uses RobotOps coordinate space
Add validation and test utilities for transforms
Create admin UI in Settings for managing map mappings and transforms
transform_rules: input point in vendor coordinates becomes output point in RobotOps floorplan coordinates
apply scale first, then rotation, then translation
rotation is around origin of vendor frame unless specified otherwise
store all transform parameters per vendor map mapping
acceptance_criteria: Robot_state ingest with vendor map id is transformed into RobotOps floorplan coordinates
Facility map displays robots correctly on the chosen floorplan
Transform tests cover scale only, rotation only, translation only, and combined transforms

phases.name: Harden dedupe and ordering rules
phases.goal: Prevent duplicates and reduce jitter by defining deterministic ordering, late data handling, and dedupe windows for each message type.
deliverables: Define dedupe keys and windows for robot_event and task_status
Define ordering rules per robot for robot_state with out of order dropping
Add message sequence support where available
Add storage for last processed sequence and timestamp per robot and per task
Ensure incident list and mission timeline do not duplicate entries

robot_state.dedupe: last write wins by timestamp, drop if older than last_state_timestamp minus allowed_lateness_seconds
robot_state.ordering: prefer sequence if provided, else timestamp

robot_event.dedupe: dedupe_key required, unique on tenant_id site_id robot_id dedupe_key within window

task_status.dedupe: unique on tenant_id site_id task_id state updated_at within window
task_status.persistence: append only task status store and also update task_last_status read model

acceptance_criteria: Duplicate robot_event payloads do not create duplicate incidents
Out of order robot_state updates do not move robot backwards in time in the UI
Task timeline does not show duplicate state transitions
All message types are idempotent using message_id and dedupe rules

phases.name: Adapter interface plus replay and recording harness
phases.goal: Create a robust integration development workflow by adding adapter contracts, raw payload recording, and deterministic replay through the same code path into ingest.
deliverables: Define adapter interface for polling adapters and streaming adapters
Implement recorder to persist raw vendor payloads to disk or object storage with metadata
Implement replayer that replays recorded payloads through adapter to produce canonical envelopes
Add CLI commands to record, replay, and validate streams
Add integration test suite that replays canned streams end to end

methods: poll() returns list of canonical envelopes
on_message(raw_payload) returns zero or more canonical envelopes

acceptance_criteria: You can record vendor payloads and replay them to produce identical canonical envelopes
Replay can run in CI with deterministic results
Adapter health endpoint reports last success and last error

phases.name: Optimize Socket.io live updates using diffs and cursors
phases.goal: Reduce bandwidth and server load by moving from broadcasting full arrays to broadcasting deltas based on cursors and changed entities since a checkpoint time.
deliverables: Add cursor based subscription protocol for clients
Emit robot_last_state deltas as robots change, not full robot arrays
Emit incident and mission changes as event driven updates
Add server side coalescing to batch rapid state updates
Update client to reconcile updates into local state using ids
performance_targets: Avoid broadcasting full robot arrays every tick
Support thousands of robots with stable CPU and memory
Keep UI responsive with reconciliation on client
acceptance_criteria: Client receives only deltas after initial load
Fleet page updates smoothly without full refresh payloads
Bandwidth drops significantly compared to full array broadcasts
Server batches rapid updates without losing correctness

notes: Phase 1 to 4 create stable ingestion semantics for any vendor integration
Phase 5 makes integration development safe and repeatable
Phase 6 keeps UI and infrastructure cost sane as you scale

no_regressions: Existing pages still function with dummy data
RBAC and tenant isolation remain enforced
